name: linkedin-truncation-applied
prompt: Write a LinkedIn post from me (the founder) announcing that Loopwork raised a $2.4M seed round led by Foundry Capital — I don't want the post to get cut off awkwardly in the feed.
expected_output: A ready-to-post LinkedIn announcement whose hook and key news land before the ~210-character truncation point, using only the provided facts.
assertions: The announcement (the raise) lands within roughly the first 210 characters, before LinkedIn's '...see more' truncation
Shows awareness of the truncation point explicitly or structurally (front-loaded news, short lines, white space)
Delivers an actual ready-to-post LinkedIn post
Uses the provided facts ($2.4M, Foundry Capital) and invents no additional stats, customers, or metrics
